senses.definition: Plurals of rooser, a slang term for someone who rooses (not a standard English word; may be a misspelling or nonce formation).
examples: The text used the name 'roosers' without clear meaning.
In the slang dictionary, rooser isn't widely recognized.

senses.definition: Plural form of roose (not a standard English word); likely a misspelling or nonce term.
examples: rooses appears in the text but has no established meaning.

senses.definition: The surname of American presidents Franklin D. Roosevelt and Theodore Roosevelt; used as a proper noun for the family or as a reference to the presidents.
examples: Roosevelt led the United States during the Great Depression.
The Roosevelt family has a long political history.

senses.definition: Relating to Franklin D. Roosevelt or his policies; characteristic of his style or era.
examples: Rooseveltian economics emphasized public works and social programs.
The speech had a Rooseveltian optimism.

senses.definition: Present participle of roose (not standard); not established in contemporary English.
examples: The term roosing appears in minor or humorous texts.

senses.definition: A place where birds rest or sleep, such as a perch or small building for birds.
examples: The hen climbed back to the roost at dusk.
We watched the birds settle on the roosts.

senses.definition: To settle or rest like birds on a roost; to perch.
examples: The chickens roosted for the night.
The pigeons roost in the old tree.

senses.definition: Past tense of roost; settled or slept on a perch or resting place.
examples: The hens roosted early after the storm.
Birds roosted high in the tree.

senses.definition: A male chicken; often kept for breeding or as a watchdog in some settings.
examples: The rooster crowed at dawn.
Families keep a rooster in the yard.

senses.definition: A large tropical fish, known for its elongated dorsal fin; named for its rooster-like appearance.
examples: The diver spotted a roosterfish near the coral reef.

senses.definition: The state or condition of being a rooster; a humorous or informal term.
examples: The farm celebrated the roosterhood of the prized rooster.

senses.definition: Without a rooster; lacking a male chicken.
examples: The farm keeps a roosterless hen pen.

senses.definition: Plural of rooster; two or more male chickens.
examples: The yard had several roosters.

senses.definition: The status or condition of having a roost; a humorous or informal coinage.
examples: He spoke about his roostership within the flock.

senses.definition: Present participle of roost; the act of birds settling or sleeping on a perch.
examples: Birds were roosting in the barn at sunset.
The chickens are roosting for the night.

senses.definition: Places where birds rest or sleep; perches or nests.
examples: The trees are full of roosts for the night.
We counted several roosts along the fence.

senses.definition: Having many roosts; possibly a humorous or informal coinage referring to perching areas.
examples: The roosty branches attracted many birds.

senses.definition: The part of a plant that grows downward into the soil and supports the plant; also the source or origin of something.
examples: The plant's roots are deep in the soil.
Explore the root of the problem.

senses.definition: To establish the basis or origin of something; to base on or derive from.
examples: These beliefs root in ancient traditions.
New policies root in data gathered last year.

senses.definition: A relatively rare or technical term referring to the act or condition of rooting; often used in botany.
examples: The rootage of the cuttings was encouraging.

senses.definition: Plural of rootage; multiple instances or measures of rooting.
examples: The rootages of several cuttings were monitored.

senses.definition: A mass of soil and roots from a plant or tree, typically kept intact when transplanting.
examples: We lifted the rootball carefully to transplant the shrub.
The rootball held together during the move.

senses.definition: Plural of rootball; multiple masses of soil and roots.
examples: The garden had several rootballs ready for planting.

senses.definition: A condition in which a plant's roots have grown in circles and are crowded, often harming growth.
examples: The ficus tree became rootbound in its small pot.
Repotting can help if a plant is rootbound.

senses.definition: A protective cap at the tip of a plant root or an analogous term in other contexts.
examples: Rootcap protects the growing tip as the root penetrates the soil.

senses.definition: Plural of rootcap; multiple protective tips of roots.
examples: Different species have varying rootcaps.

senses.definition: Having established roots; deeply ingrained or firmly established.
examples: The tradition is rooted in ancient culture.
Her beliefs are deeply rooted in family values.
